page: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz and includes 48 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates solid general-purpose performance across various workloads. The CPU achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48013.03 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15152.0, both falling within the average range. It demonstrates strong capabilities in specific tasks, such as image processing with a Geekbench score of 14757.0 and CPU encryption with a Passmark score of 42240.46, both ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is also strong, with a Passmark memory score of 2788.8 and a cached read-only small block memory bandwidth of 2502.98 GB/sec.

Due to the lack of local NVMe storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is not designed for high-I/O database systems requiring local scratch space or heavy parallelized graphics processing. Large LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with text generation on a 70B model yielding a weak performance of 2.62 tokens/sec. However, with its balanced resource allocation, it is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving (demonstrated by a static web serving throughput of 36.47 GB/sec), software compilation, and medium-scale database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the D48_v5 in the average tier for general compute, with a Passmark CPU score of 48013.03 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15152.0. However, it displays strong performance in memory operations, image processing, and encryption tasks. Lacking local NVMe storage, it is best suited for workloads relying on external storage, such as web serving, application servers, software compilation, and standard database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for enterprise applications and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs and robust memory bandwidth for enterprise application hosting and web serving.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Strong performance in memory bandwidth, image processing, and encryption benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and general enterprise applications
